titel: Identity requirements for property registration and measures to prevent circumvention of the condominium law.
html:
The government proposes measures aimed at combating crime in the real estate market and preventing circumvention of legislation regarding condominiums.

It is proposed that the requirements for providing identification information in land registration applications be tightened. Anyone acquiring a property must always provide a personal identification number, a coordination number, or an organizational number. The purpose is to create better control over who owns a property. Increased requirements for identification information can contribute to the police and other authorities' ability to prevent and combat crime.

Measures are also proposed to prevent circumvention of legislation when rental properties are converted into condominiums. To ensure that only those who actually live in the property vote on a conversion, only those who have been registered as residents at the property for at least six months immediately preceding the association meeting where the decision is made should be allowed to participate in the vote.

It is also proposed that the same rules that apply when converting from a rental property to a condominium should apply when an economic association is reregistered as a condominium association.

The proposed amendments to the law are scheduled to come into effect on July 1, 2026.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:106

Government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Land Code.
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Condominium Act (1991:614).

3.

Prop. 2025/26:106 2
tldr: The government wants to change the laws to prevent fraud in real estate transactions and make it more difficult to cheat during the conversion of rental properties to owner-occupied housing. The proposal would require individuals to provide clearer identification when purchasing a property.
lattlast:
The government wants to introduce new regulations to reduce crime in the real estate market. When applying for a property deed, which is the official proof of ownership, applicants must always provide their personal identification number, coordination number, or company registration number. This will make it easier for the police and other authorities to verify who actually owns a property.

It will also become more difficult to circumvent the rules when rental apartments are converted into condominiums. To ensure that those who actually live in the property have the deciding power, the government proposes that individuals must be registered as residents at the address for at least six months before they are allowed to vote. Being registered as a resident means being officially registered at the address in the population registry.

The same rules will also apply when an economic association is converted into a condominium association. The purpose is to ensure that the rules are equal and fair in both cases.

If the Parliament votes in favor of the proposal, which is outlined in document 2025/26:106, the new laws will come into effect on July 1, 2026.
